name: doc-sync-tool
description: 自动同步项目中的 Agents.md、claude.md 和 gemini.md 文件，保持内容一致性。支持自动监听和手动触发。
capabilities: Recursively scans project directories to discover AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, and GEMINI.md files
Automatically creates missing documentation files by copying content from the most recently modified source
Real-time file watching with debouncing to detect and sync changes instantly
Excludes common directories like node_modules, .git, .next, dist, and .obsidian
Supports both manual sync execution and continuous background monitoring via PM2
Provides colored terminal output with detailed sync status and statistics
author: ZhanlinCui
securitySummary: Static analysis flagged 254 external_commands patterns and 31 filesystem operations, but evaluation confirms these are false positives. The 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' findings are markdown documentation examples in EXAMPLES.md, INDEX.md, README.md, QUICKSTART.md, and SKILL.md - not executable code. The 'hex-encoded characters' and 'weak cryptographic algorithm' findings are ANSI terminal color codes (e.g., \x1b[32m for green). The tool performs legitimate file synchronization using Node.js fs module with proper path handling via path.join() and path.dirname(). One minor concern: rename-quick.js uses child_process.execSync with hardcoded 'find' commands, which is acceptable but should be noted.
valueStatement: Maintaining identical configuration files for multiple AI agents is tedious and error-prone. This tool automatically keeps AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, and GEMINI.md files in sync across your entire project with real-time file watching or manual triggering.
limitations: Requires Node.js 14 or higher runtime environment
File watching may have limited support on some network filesystems
Does not handle merge conflicts - always overwrites with the newest file content
Cannot sync files across different directories, only within the same folder

useCases.targetUser: Developers starting projects with multi-agent support
useCases.title: New Project Setup
useCases.description: When starting a new project, create just one agent config file (e.g., CLAUDE.md) and let the tool automatically generate AGENTS.md and GEMINI.md with identical content.

useCases.targetUser: Teams maintaining documentation for Claude, Gemini, and other AI assistants
useCases.title: Continuous Documentation Sync
useCases.description: Run the tool in watch mode during development to automatically propagate any agent configuration changes to all other agent files in real-time.

useCases.targetUser: Project managers coordinating documentation across repositories
useCases.title: Bulk Synchronization
useCases.description: Execute manual sync across multiple project directories at once to ensure all agent documentation stays consistent after major updates.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Setup
promptTemplates.prompt: Set up automatic documentation sync for my project. I have a CLAUDE.md file and need to create matching AGENTS.md and GEMINI.md files.
promptTemplates.scenario: First-time user wants to initialize sync

promptTemplates.title: Manual Sync Execution
promptTemplates.prompt: Run the doc-sync-tool to synchronize all agent documentation files in the current project directory and show me which files were updated.
promptTemplates.scenario: User made changes and wants to sync manually

promptTemplates.title: Background Monitoring
promptTemplates.prompt: Start the file watcher in background mode using PM2 so my agent docs stay synchronized automatically. Show me how to verify it is running.

promptTemplates.title: Configuration Customization
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me modify the doc-sync-tool configuration to exclude additional directories and change the target file names to match my project conventions.

bestPractices: Run the tool in watch mode during active development to keep all agent docs synchronized in real-time
Use PM2 for production environments to ensure the file watcher survives system restarts
Commit all three documentation files to version control for complete backup and history tracking
antiPatterns: Do not manually edit multiple agent files simultaneously - let the sync tool handle propagation
Avoid running multiple sync instances on the same directory to prevent race conditions
Do not exclude directories that actually contain agent documentation you want synchronized

faq.question: What files does this tool synchronize?
faq.answer: The tool synchronizes three files: AGENTS.md (or Agents.md), CLAUDE.md (or claude.md), and GEMINI.md (or gemini.md). It matches files case-insensitively within the same directory.

faq.question: How does the tool decide which file is the source?
faq.answer: The tool uses modification time (mtime) to determine the source. The most recently modified file becomes the source, and its content is copied to the other files.

faq.question: Can I customize which files are synchronized?
faq.answer: Yes. Edit the targetFiles array in sync.js to change which filenames are synchronized. The default is ['AGENTS.md', 'CLAUDE.md', 'GEMINI.md'].

faq.question: Does the watch mode consume a lot of system resources?
faq.answer: No. The watch mode uses Node.js native fs.watch with recursive monitoring. It consumes less than 50MB of memory and near-zero CPU when idle.

faq.question: How do I stop the file watcher?
faq.answer: Press Ctrl+C in the terminal to stop. If running via PM2, use 'pm2 stop doc-sync' or 'pm2 delete doc-sync' to stop and remove the service.

faq.question: What happens if two files are modified at the same time?
faq.answer: The tool includes debouncing to handle rapid changes. It will use whichever file has the most recent modification timestamp after the debounce period completes.
